name: Peking Lamb with Leeks
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces lamb fillet, cut into broad slices
4 cups vegetable oil, for deep frying, plus 1 tablespoon for stir-frying
1 large leek, white and part of green thinly sliced
1/2 cup dried black Chinese mushrooms, soaked for 15 minutes in water
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl combine lamb with baking soda and 1/4 cup cold water. Mix well and soak for 5 hours.
2. Rinse lamb slices and dry well on paper towels.
3. In a small bowl mix together egg white, salt, cornstarch, 1 teaspoon of the garlic, peanut oil, and 1 tablespoon of Shao Hsing. Add lamb and toss to coat with marinade.
4. In another small bowl combine brown bean sauce, remaining 3 tablespoons Shao Hsing, sugar, monosodium glutamate, stock, vinegar, and sesame oil. Mix cornstarch with a little cold water to the consistency of heavy cream and whisk into the bean sauce. Set aside.
5. In a first wok heat 4 cups oil over high heat to 375 degrees. Drain lamb, pat dry, and deep-fry for 1 minute, turning pieces several times with a metal spoon. Use a slotted spoon to remove lamb to paper towels. Return lamb to the oil and deep-fry for another 1 minute; remove and drain again. Return lamb a third time and deep-fry for 30 seconds more; remove and drain on clean paper towels.
6. In a second wok heat remaining 1 tablespoon oil over high heat. Add leek, remaining garlic, and chili peppers to taste; stir-fry for 30 seconds. Add mushrooms and lamb and mix well. Stir the reserved bean sauce mixture and add to the wok, tossing to combine. Bring liquids to a boil and remove from heat as soon as the sauce begins to thicken.
7. Serve immediately with rice.
Yield: 3 to 4 servings as part of a Chinese meal
